question: Which university did the creator of the Python programming language attend?

paragraphs.title: Python (programming language)
paragraphs.paragraph_text: Python is a high-level, general-purpose programming language. Its design philosophy emphasizes code readability with the use of significant indentation. Python was created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991.

paragraphs.title: Guido van Rossum
paragraphs.paragraph_text: Guido van Rossum is a Dutch programmer best known as the creator of the Python programming language. He attended the University of Amsterdam, where he received a master's degree in mathematics and computer science in 1982.

paragraphs.title: Oppenheimer (film)
paragraphs.paragraph_text: Oppenheimer is a 2023 biographical drama film written and directed by Christopher Nolan. The film won seven Academy Awards at the 96th Oscars, including the prestigious Best Picture award.

question: In which city is the university located where one of the co-authors of the Transformer architecture completed his PhD?

paragraphs.title: Transformer (deep learning model)
paragraphs.paragraph_text: The Transformer is a deep learning architecture introduced in 2017 by researchers at Google. The seminal paper Attention Is All You Need was co-authored by Ashish Vaswani, Noam Shazeer, Niki Parmar, and Jakob Uszkoreit.

paragraphs.title: Ashish Vaswani
paragraphs.paragraph_text: Ashish Vaswani is a computer scientist known for his work in deep learning. He completed his PhD in Computer Science at the University of Southern California (USC) before joining Google Brain as a research scientist.

paragraphs.title: University of Southern California
paragraphs.paragraph_text: The University of Southern California (USC) is a private research university in Los Angeles, California. Founded in 1880, it is the oldest private research university in California.

paragraphs.title: University of Amsterdam
paragraphs.paragraph_text: The University of Amsterdam is a public research university located in Amsterdam, Netherlands. Established in 1632, it is the third-oldest university in the Netherlands.

question: Who directed the movie that won the Academy Award for Best Picture in 2023?

paragraphs.title: Oppenheimer (film)
paragraphs.paragraph_text: Oppenheimer is a 2023 biographical drama film written and directed by Christopher Nolan. The film won seven Academy Awards at the 96th Oscars, including the prestigious Best Picture award.

paragraphs.title: Christopher Nolan
paragraphs.paragraph_text: Christopher Nolan is a British-American filmmaker known for his Hollywood blockbusters with complex storytelling. He directed critically acclaimed films like Inception, Interstellar, and Oppenheimer.
